content:
AI writes the code in seconds. The question nobody answers: is it actually right?

I'm building The Loupe — short, real-world exercises where you verify AI-written code before you ship it. The dangerous bugs aren't the crashes; they're the lines that look right and quietly aren't.

A well-known Korean dev creator (Zerocho) just featured it in a short video, which made my week 🙏

▶ The 60-second idea: https://youtube.com/shorts/0xcYQquVZp0?si=nbydJs49F6Vyk5Vp

Would love feedback from this community — what's the sneakiest "looks-right-but-wrong" bug you've shipped?
